title: Constructors - setting an object up at birth
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A constructor is a special method that runs automatically when an object is created, to set its initial state. It has the same name as the class and no return type.
explanation: You don't want a half-built object floating around. A constructor guarantees setup: it shares the class's name, takes whatever it needs, and runs the moment you create an object - Point p(3, 4) calls it with 3 and 4. After it finishes, the object is ready and valid. It's the class's promise that every one of its objects starts life correctly.

exercises.question: When does a constructor run?
options: only if you call it by name

exercises.question: What name does a constructor have?
options: the same name as the class
